repo.name: RiftScan
repo.preferred_workflow: RiftScan Operator GUI first; manual PowerShell only if Operator fails

patch_runner_boundaries: fails cleanly on no manifest, parse error, or validation error
stops before extraction or patch execution

architecture_decision.decision: Prefer on-demand Operator workflow before any always-on listener/server.
architecture_decision.reason: Lower risk, easier debugging, no background repo mutation, explicit operator control.
architecture_decision.cloud_boundary: ChatGPT can design/review code and analyze artifacts; local Windows/RIFT actions must be run by local tools on Joey's PC.

future_plan: Freeze and preserve the current patch-runner alpha2 evidence.
Disable or avoid any always-on patcher service behavior for now.
Implement Operator on-demand Check Online Patch Inbox as discovery-only first.
Add package download/hash validation only after discovery is boring and reliable.
Add staging extraction only after validation is proven.
Add patch dry-run only after staging is proven.
Add real patch application only with explicit GUI approval and rollback metadata.
rollback_recommendation: Soft rollback service/listener path to on-demand Operator-only workflow; keep alpha2 manifest validator unless specifically rejected.
explicit_no_go_for_now: no automatic extraction into live repo
no arbitrary shell commands from repo manifests
no force-push rollback unless intentionally chosen as a last resort
